rationale: This archetype is a documentary-style cross-section reveal of real-world landmarks — visually (muted navy/orange/forest palette, architectural illustration) and tonally (slow curiosity pacing) it's the opposite of Push'em All's bright, kinetic stickman physics gameplay. There's no hook bridge to swiping, pushing crowds, or platform falls, so adapting it would require fabricating a contrived "what's beneath the platform" angle that abandons the proven satisfying-push payoff. Audience overlap exists only loosely (broad casual viewers), and the archetype's tiny sample (1 creative, 1 advertiser, 328d old) signals this is a weak, stale signal — do not prioritize testing.
